message:
perf(extensions): pre-compute tool defs and command names in RCU snapshot to eliminate mutex contention

The extension system's hot-path methods (extension_tool_defs, has_command,
extension_providers) previously acquired the inner mutex on every call, then
iterated all extensions and cloned results. Under concurrent agent load this
created lock contention and redundant allocations.

**RegistrySnapshot enhancements (src/extensions.rs):**
- Added all_tool_defs: Vec<Value> — flat pre-computed tool definitions from all extensions
- Added command_names: HashSet<String> — normalized command names for O(1) has_command()
- Implemented precompute_all_tool_defs() and precompute_command_names() methods
- extension_tool_defs() now reads from snapshot (RCU) instead of locking mutex
- has_command() now uses HashSet::contains() instead of iterating all extensions
- extension_providers() now reads from snapshot instead of locking mutex

**String allocation reduction (src/extensions.rs):**
- precompute_all_flags(): HashSet<String> → HashSet<&str> (borrows instead of cloning)
- precompute_all_event_hooks(): same &str borrowing optimization
- Avoids intermediate String allocations during dedup passes

**HostcallTracker micro-optimization (src/extensions_js.rs):**
- Reordered HashMap insertions in register() so final insert consumes call_id
- Eliminates one String::clone() per hostcall registration

**New unit tests (src/extensions.rs, src/extension_dispatcher.rs):**
- host_result_to_outcome: error-without-payload defaults to "internal"/"Unknown error"
- host_result_to_outcome: chunk field takes precedence over is_error flag
- Error code normalization tests: mixed-case handling across all HostCallErrorCode variants

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
